title: Flaky Warm German Bread Baedekas
description: A spicy, flaky, and cozy German bread dish featuring apple juice and steam, gluten-free-friendly.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the Gluten-free all-purpose flour, warm water, active dry yeast, and salt. Mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Add the applesauce, apple juice, unsalted butter, sesame seeds, paprika, cayenne pepper, and sugar to the dough. Mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and shape it into a round loaf. Place the loaf on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the bread for 25 minutes, or until golden brown.
During the last 5 minutes of baking, pour 1 cup of apple juice over the bread to create a steamed crust.
Remove the bread from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes.
Wrap the bread in a clean towel and steam for 5 minutes by pouring 1 cup of boiling water into a small saucepan and placing the bread over the steam.
Slice the bread and serve warm.
